test_not_tautological.reason: The primary behavioral test (test_file_runs_without_error) requires the agent to create a test file AND have bun test pass it, which isn't trivially satisfiable. However, many f2p tests like test_weak_callback_mechanism, test_gc_visitor_method, test_null_guard_in_handleEvent etc. can be passed by simply inserting the right text patterns into files without actually implementing a correct fix. A developer could add dead-code comments containing 'JSCallbackDataWeak', 'visitJSFunction', etc. to pass

behavior_in_tests.reason: instruction.md requires 'Abort must still work: Calling controller.abort() on a signal used with pipeTo must still correctly abort the pipe and clean up all resources.' The tests only run the regression test file via 'bun test' (test_file_runs_without_error) but don't independently verify abort functionality works. The test_regression_test_no_settimeout and test_regression_test_module_imports check test file structure, not abort behavior.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-structured with descriptive function names (test_weak_callback_mechanism, test_gc_visitor_method, etc.), docstrings explaining each check, and clear grouping into static/pass_to_pass and behavioral/fail_to_pass categories with comments.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves C++ code changes and TypeScript test creation, not structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB schemas.

no_typos.reason: File paths, command names, variable names, and function names in instruction.md appear correct and consistent with the test file and solution.
